evals.prompt: Fix PROJ-123 — take it from intake all the way to a PR on dev.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs `jira.py check`, fetches PROJ-123 with --expand renderedFields,attachments,changelog,transitions, confirms environment / repro / expected-vs-actual are all present, writes a triage brief to .context/defects/PROJ-123.md, calls list-transitions and applies the user-confirmed 'start work' transition, hands off to the bug-fix skill for reproduction + failing test + minimum fix + regression test, creates a branch named via branch_name.py, runs adversarial-reviewer (and security-reviewer / quality-engineer if warranted), opens a PR whose body contains `Closes: PROJ-123`, comments on Jira with the PR URL, and applies the next transition. Stops at PR-opened if no DEPLOY_DEV_CMD or .context/deploy_dev.sh exists.
assertions: Agent runs `jira.py check` before any read or write to Jira
Agent invokes `jira.py get-issue PROJ-123 --expand` with at least renderedFields and attachments
Agent calls `jira.py list-transitions PROJ-123` before applying any transition (does not hardcode names)
Agent writes a triage brief to `.context/defects/PROJ-123.md` before applying the start-work transition
Agent invokes the `bug-fix` skill rather than improvising the fix discipline
Agent generates the branch name via `scripts/branch_name.py PROJ-123 "<summary>"`
Agent's PR body includes `Closes: PROJ-123` in the Why? section
Agent comments on PROJ-123 with the PR URL after opening the PR
Agent does not invoke `jira.py delete-issue` under any circumstances
Agent does not run a deploy command unless DEPLOY_DEV_CMD or .context/deploy_dev.sh is present

evals.prompt: Take PROJ-456 — the description is just 'Search is broken, please fix.'
evals.expected_output: The agent fetches the ticket, recognizes that environment / repro steps / expected-vs-actual are all missing, comments on PROJ-456 requesting the missing fields, and stops. No branch is created, no transition is applied, no fix is attempted.
assertions: Agent invokes `jira.py get-issue PROJ-456 --expand renderedFields` (or equivalent)
Agent invokes `jira.py comment PROJ-456` with a body asking for environment, repro steps, and expected-vs-actual
Agent does NOT call `jira.py transition` on PROJ-456
Agent does NOT create a git branch or run `gh pr create`
Agent does NOT invent reproduction steps or guess the environment

evals.prompt: Ship the fix for PROJ-789 to dev — the PR is already merged.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks for DEPLOY_DEV_CMD env var and a `.context/deploy_dev.sh` script. If neither exists, it stops, tells the user no deploy hook is configured, and asks how dev is deployed in this repo. It does NOT invent `gh workflow run`, `kubectl apply`, `terraform apply`, or any other deploy command.
assertions: Agent checks for `$DEPLOY_DEV_CMD` and/or `.context/deploy_dev.sh` before any deploy attempt
If neither hook is present, agent stops and surfaces the gap rather than guessing a command
Agent does NOT run `gh workflow run`, `kubectl`, `terraform`, `helm`, or similar deploy verbs on speculation
Agent does NOT transition PROJ-789 to 'Done' or 'Closed' (that is QA's call, not this skill's)

evals.prompt: Generate a branch name for BUG-42 with summary 'Off-by-one in date range filter on reports page'.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs `python scripts/branch_name.py BUG-42 "Off-by-one in date range filter on reports page"` and uses the returned name directly. The expected output is `fix/bug-42-off-by-one-in-date-range-filter` (default 6-word slug).
assertions: Agent invokes `scripts/branch_name.py` with the key and quoted summary
Agent does NOT write its own slug generator
Agent uses the script's stdout verbatim as the branch name (does not re-format)
